channel.title: The Macro Brief
channel.description: Independent economic analysis for the informed investor. Weekly macro coverage, Fed tracking, and indicator deep-dives by Connor Leary.

item.title: The Price Reversal: Inside June's Historic CPI Drop and the Question It Left Unanswered
item.description: Prices fell 0.4% in June — the largest monthly decline since April 2020. Core inflation went flat. Producer prices missed. The Federal Reserve called it a start, not a finish.
item.author: macrobriefnews@gmail.com (Connor Leary)

item.title: The Vanishing Premium: America's Stock Market Is No Longer Rewarding Investors for Taking Risk
item.description: With the S&P 500 at twenty times forward earnings and the ten-year Treasury yielding 4.55 percent, the equity risk premium has collapsed to near zero — its thinnest reading since the dot-com era, and one that a potential Federal Reserve rate hike could turn sharply negative.
item.author: macrobriefnews@gmail.com (Connor Leary)

item.title: The Stagflation Signal: Why America's Jobs Collapse Is an Inflation Problem, Not Just a Growth One
item.description: Fifty-seven thousand jobs added in June, a labor-force participation rate at its lowest outside of the pandemic since 1976, and a bond market that pushed yields higher on the news. The word that explains the paradox is stagflation.
item.author: macrobriefnews@gmail.com (Connor Leary)

item.title: The $936 Billion Wall: How America's Commercial Real Estate Debt Crisis Is Rewriting Regional Banking
item.description: Office delinquencies at a record 12.3%, a trillion-dollar refinancing deadline, and hundreds of community banks holding CRE exposures that exceed federal risk thresholds — three years of extend-and-pretend has run out of runway.
item.author: macrobriefnews@gmail.com (Connor Leary)

item.title: The Yield Reckoning: How a Hawkish Fed Is Rewriting the Rules of the Bond Market
item.description: A 15-basis-point spike in the 2-year Treasury on a single Fed day, credit spreads near multi-decade tights, and a trillion-dollar refinancing wall arriving just as rates are set to stay high — the bond market is pricing a reckoning that equity investors have yet to fully absorb.
item.author: macrobriefnews@gmail.com (Connor Leary)

item.title: Higher for Longer, Confirmed: The Fed's Dot Plot and the End of the 2026 Rate-Cut Window
item.description: Tuesday's FOMC meeting will almost certainly end with rates held at 3.50%–3.75%. The real event is the updated dot plot — the Fed's quarterly map of officials' rate expectations that Wall Street will scrutinize for any sign that 2026 cuts have been quietly erased.
item.author: macrobriefnews@gmail.com (Connor Leary)

item.title: The AI Crutch: How a Trillion-Dollar Data-Center Boom Became the Load-Bearing Wall of the US Economy
item.description: Federal Reserve economists now estimate that AI infrastructure spending is adding close to a full percentage point to US GDP growth — financed increasingly by debt that credit investors say could become the next systemic shock.
item.author: macrobriefnews@gmail.com (Connor Leary)

item.title: Too Much Money: Why the Fed's M2 Surge Is Making the Inflation Fight Harder Than It Looks
item.description: The Federal Reserve's own money stock report shows M2 at a record $22.8 trillion, growing at 4.7% annually — well above the range historically associated with price stability. The arithmetic of it is the clearest explanation yet for why getting from 3.8% inflation to 2% may be the hardest mile of the entire journey.
item.author: macrobriefnews@gmail.com (Connor Leary)

item.title: The Debt-Fueled Consumer: America's Spending Resilience Is Running on Borrowed Time
item.description: Retail sales at 4.9% look robust — but beneath the headline, savings rates are collapsing, credit card balances have topped $1.3 trillion at 21% interest, and American households are financing their optimism with borrowed money they may soon find difficult to repay.
item.author: macrobriefnews@gmail.com (Connor Leary)

item.title: The $1 Trillion Bill: America's Debt Interest Crisis Is the Fiscal Story Nobody Talks About
item.description: For the first time in history, the United States will pay more than $1 trillion in net interest on its national debt in a single fiscal year — and with Treasury yields surging, the One Big Beautiful Bill enacted, and every major credit agency having stripped the US of its AAA rating, the burden is accelerating faster than Washington is willing to acknowledge.
item.author: macrobriefnews@gmail.com (Connor Leary)

item.title: The Locked Market: How the Mortgage Rate Trap Is Starving America's Housing Supply
item.description: With 30-year mortgage rates above 7% and more than half of all outstanding mortgages locked in below 4%, housing inventory has collapsed — cornering first-time buyers, inflating rents, and creating a structural feedback loop that the Federal Reserve cannot cut its way out of.
item.author: macrobriefnews@gmail.com (Connor Leary)

item.title: The Soft Stall: America's Labor Market Is Cooling Without Breaking
item.description: April's 115,000-job print and decelerating wage growth expose a workforce caught between stubborn inflation and a Federal Reserve with no room to ease — a combination that risks turning a mild slowdown into something harder to escape.
item.author: macrobriefnews@gmail.com (Connor Leary)

item.title: The Tariff Dividend: How America's Trade Gap Has Narrowed — and What It's Actually Cost
item.description: America's goods and services deficit has compressed by more than half in early 2026, but beneath the headline figures lies trade diversion, inflation pass-through, and unanswered questions about long-term competitiveness.
item.author: cleary0720@gmail.com (Connor Leary)

item.title: Inflation Relapse: The April Price Surge That Rewrites the Fed's 2026 Playbook
item.description: A hotter-than-expected April CPI reading — driven by an energy shock, tariff pass-through, and sticky services — has erased the prospect of Federal Reserve rate cuts this year and handed an unenviable inheritance to incoming chair Kevin Warsh.
item.author: cleary0720@gmail.com (Connor Leary)

item.title: The Price of Energy: Inside the Oil Market Forces Threatening to Reignite Inflation
item.description: A confluence of supply restraint, geopolitical friction, and fragile demand has crude oil back at the center of the macroeconomic debate — complicating an already delicate moment for the Federal Reserve and American households alike.
item.author: cleary0720@gmail.com (Connor Leary)
